title: Add try/catch around gateway calls in repository fallback chains
description:
## Council-promoted to P0

## Evidence
`MemorySmithBlueprintRepository.GetAsync()` and `MemorySmithItemRegistry.FetchAsync()` call `memory.GetPageAsync()` without try/catch. A transport exception (HTTP 500, timeout) bypasses the local file fallback and search fallback entirely.

## Impact
A temporary backend outage makes blueprints and items disappear even when local file copies exist in `Data/Pages/`.

## Design decisions needed
1. Catch `HttpRequestException` and `TaskCanceledException` (timeout) only
2. `OperationCanceledException` from CancellationToken should propagate, not be swallowed
3. Only fall back on transport/network exceptions — not on "not found" (empty content)

## Fix
1. Wrap gateway lookups in narrow try/catch catching HttpRequestException/TaskCanceledException
2. On transport failure, continue to local file fallback (LoadLocalPage / search)
3. Log the exception type and page/item ID
4. Only fall back on transport/network exceptions (not on "not found")
5. Add tests: gateway throws → fallback succeeds; gateway returns empty → returns null

## Scope
Both files: `Agent.Memory/MemorySmithBlueprintRepository.GetAsync()` and `Agent.Memory/MemorySmithItemRegistry.FetchAsync()`

## Verification
Verified against codebase 2026-06-23. Council: `Data/Pages/council/audit-task-prioritization-council-2026-06-24.md`
